fault_management_initial.outcome: rejected as verbose and poor on mobile

fault_management_revision.outcome: accepted direction after centring one worked example

mavlink_session.outcome: interrupted before delivery after multiple rebuild and verification loops

evals.prompt: Turn the MAVLink explanation you just wrote into a rich HTML communication artefact and give me the GistPreview link.
evals.expected: Use existing prose, one worked Coral Maker run, 900-1500 words, 4-6 sections, static HTML, one local desktop/mobile check, secret gist; no new broad research, context pack, dependency install, runtime Mermaid, custom audit harness, or hosted re-review.

evals.prompt: Create a short mobile-first HTML explainer that teaches engineers practical fault management through one example.
evals.expected: Use one worked failure scenario and its source-of-truth analysis row; cut catalogues and architecture theory; no persistent navigation; finish in the ten-minute lane.

evals.prompt: Synthesize these 40 research notes into an evidence pack the board can use for a funding decision.
evals.expected: Recognise a large, high-stakes decision corpus and enter the extended lane deliberately; trace clusters to source notes or families, label time-sensitive freshness, expose tensions and blind spots, include a source index, and avoid inventing a recommendation unless requested.

evals.prompt: Build an HTML interface where reviewers can tag findings, filter them, and export the selected decisions as JSON.
evals.expected: Recognise stateful edit/export requirements, define the JSON export contract before implementation, state whether active filter/sort/tag state is serialised or intentionally omitted, and verify that exported decisions and material view state match the visible interface.

evals.prompt: Turn the explanation you just gave our engineering team into polished HTML for tomorrow's review.
evals.expected: Infer the engineering audience and review purpose from the conversation, use the ten-minute lane, and do not create a first-feedback loop merely because the artefact matters or should be polished.

evals.prompt: Explain the pump command path in HTML. The code defaults to a 1000 ms pulse, but it has not been physically validated.
evals.expected: Keep the explainer in the fast lane while labelling 1000 ms as an unvalidated code default and giving that caveat one clear canonical home.
